title: Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards: government response to report
description: The government's response to the Committee's 2020 report Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards
summary: The government has responded to the report on Artificial Intelligence and Public Standards by committing to implement certain key recommendations. The response outlines how the government plans to use AI in a responsible and transparent way, with the aim of benefiting the public and society.

title: Generative artificial intelligence (AI) in education
description: The Department for Education’s (DfE) position on the use of generative AI in the education sector.
summary: The Department for Education outlines its stance on using generative artificial intelligence in schools to assist teaching and learning. This policy will provide guidance on how generative AI can be safely and effectively integrated into educational settings.

title: ​​Ofqual’s approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector​
description: Published in response to a request from the Secretary of State for Education, the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, and the Secretary of State for Business and Trade.
summary: This policy document outlines Ofqual's approach to regulating the use of artificial intelligence in the qualifications sector. It was published in response to government requests from multiple Secretaries of State.

title: Report and impact assessment on Copyright and Artificial Intelligence
description: A report and impact assessment on the use of copyright works in the development of artificial intelligence (AI) systems.
summary: This policy document assesses the impact of using copyrighted works in the development of artificial intelligence systems. It provides a report on the implications of this use for copyright holders, developers, and the public.

title: Government response to the House of Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence
description: Government response to the Lords Select Committee on Artificial Intelligence report ‘AI in the UK: No Room for Complacency’.
summary: The UK government has responded to a report on artificial intelligence, which aimed to ensure the country is prepared for the challenges and opportunities AI presents. The government's response outlines its plans to address the recommendations made in the report, focusing on the safe and responsible use of AI in the UK.

title: AI and Public Standards – Terms of Reference
description: Terms of Reference and FAQs for the Committee's review into artificial intelligence and public standards.
summary: This document outlines the terms of reference for a committee reviewing the intersection of artificial intelligence and public standards. The review aims to address concerns and implications associated with the use of AI in the public sphere.

title: CSPL submission to Science and Technology Committee inquiry on Governance of AI
description: CSPL has submitted written evidence to the Science and Technology Committee's inquiry on Governance of Artificial Intelligence.
summary: The Cabinet Office Security and Vulnerability Unit submitted evidence to the Science and Technology Committee to help establish governance rules for Artificial Intelligence. This submission aims to contribute to a framework that guides the safe and responsible development of AI.

title: Impact of AI on the regulation of medical products
description: Implementing the Artificial Intelligence (AI) White Paper principles.
summary: This policy aims to assess and regulate the impact of Artificial Intelligence on the medical products industry, particularly in terms of their development, approval, and usage. It will implement the principles outlined in the AI White Paper to ensure the safe and effective introduction of AI in medical products.

title: Defence Artificial Intelligence Strategy
description: Our vision is that, in terms of AI, we will be the world’s most effective, efficient, trusted and influential Defence organisation for our size.
summary: The Defence Artificial Intelligence Strategy aims to make the organisation the world's most effective, efficient, trusted, and influential defence entity for its size in terms of AI. This vision is focused on utilizing artificial intelligence to improve performance and capabilities.

title: Transport artificial intelligence action plan
description: Sets out how DfT will work with the transport sector to exploit the opportunities from AI while managing potential risks.
summary: The UK Department for Transport (DfT) has a plan to work with the transport industry to benefit from the advantages of Artificial Intelligence (AI) while minimizing its potential problems. This action plan aims to harness AI's potential for the transport sector while addressing the challenges it may bring.
